content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 11, 1993


Kay B. Carter
Executive Director
San Diego Railroad Museum
1050 Kettner Boulevard
San Diego, California 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-93-083

Dear Ms. Carter:

You have requested advice regarding the personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),
specifically as they pertain to the use of the surplus campaign
funds of former State Senator James Mills.

You are the Executive Director of the San Diego Railroad
Museum Association ("museum"). Senator James Mills is being
considered for a paid position on the museum board. Senator Mills
has offered to donate "surplus" campaign funds to the museum.

During our telephone conversation on February 23, 1993, I
informed you that since you are not requesting advice on behalf of
Senator Mills, I could not advise you regarding the proper use of
his campaign funds.²

Furthermore, Section 89519 only governs the use of "surplus"
campaign funds raised after January 1, 1989. Elections Code
Section 12400, which is under the jurisdiction of the Attorney
General's office, governs the use of surplus campaign funds raised
prior to January 1, 1989.

_____________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 The Commission may decline to provide informal assistance or
may limit such assistance to the explanation, in general terms, of
the requirements of the Act when advice is being sought regarding
the duties of another person and the requestor does not appear to
be authorized to make the request as the person's representative.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(b)(4)(C).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-083
Page 2

I hope this is of assistance to you.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

JM:JS:dl

sections.question_synthetic: Can surplus campaign funds from former State Senator James Mills be donated to the San Diego Railroad Museum Association, and what rules govern the use of such funds?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice on behalf of Senator Mills' campaign fund usage since the requestor is not authorized to seek such advice on his behalf. The FPPC notes that Government Code Section 89519 governs surplus funds raised after January 1, 1989, while Elections Code Section 12400 (under Attorney General jurisdiction) governs surplus funds raised before that date.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. Appears to be a declination or limitation of advice letter. The FPPC provides regulatory guidance but explicitly declines to advise on the specifics of the campaign fund donation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can surplus campaign funds from former State Senator James Mills be donated to the San Diego Railroad Museum Association, and what rules govern the use of such funds?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide advice on behalf of Senator Mills' campaign fund usage since the requestor is not authorized to seek such advice on his behalf. The FPPC notes that Government Code Section 89519 governs surplus funds raised after January 1, 1989, while Elections Code Section 12400 (under Attorney General jurisdiction) governs surplus funds raised before that date.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request regarding the donation of surplus campaign funds from former State Senator James Mills to the San Diego Railroad Museum. The Commission declines to provide advice on the proper use of Senator Mills' campaign funds, as the requestor is not authorized to make the request on his behalf.
